intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Louis! Haven’t seen you in a while. How’s it been?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I Just haven’t seen you in a while, is all.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Are those bruises? On your face?

intro.speaker: Louis
intro.text: Bone-smashing. To fix my face structure.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I don’t think that’s how bones work.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: In fact I’m pretty sure that’s the opposite of how bones work.

intro.speaker: Louis
intro.text: Well what would you know? Huh?

intro.speaker: Louis
intro.text: You’ve been getting mogged and academically-cucked by Caspian for years!

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: I don’t actually know what that means, but screw you Louis, I always thought your bone structure was shit anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It wasn’t very effective

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You can’t reconfigure your face by breaking it! That doesn’t make any sense

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: What are you even trying to accomplish here?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Even if it did work I don’t see how caving your cheekbones in is gonna make you look any better.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Not that that face doesn’t need the help.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It wasn’t very effective

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Louis, have you tried going outside and talking to real people?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Maybe that’ll help with this whole bullshit you’ve got going on.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Touch come grass, witness real human interacting,

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Stop trying to make your jawline better by chewing tires.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Seriously, this is kind of pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s not very effective

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You know all these ‘looks-maxxing gurus’ are grifters right?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: They’re literally giving you your insecurities just to sell you an overpriced and ineffective solution.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You are getting conned right now.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You know, I think I remember hearing somewhere that bone-smashing only works in the sun.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: The sunlight heals your bones stronger or something.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Maybe you should take a bit of time to go outside and heal... all that.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Protein’s super bad for you, actually.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Yeah, there was a recent study, definitely legit stuff, where they found that too much protein without anything else sucks the joy out of life.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: And gives you cancer, or something.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I too have done ‘looks-maxxing’ actually.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Yeah, really, I have.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Worked pretty well, right. Come on, give me a compliment.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I mean, if you don’t admit it, that just proves it’s ineffective.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You know I do care about you, right, Louis?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re my cousin, and the youngest in the family to boot.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I’m just worried this kind of behaviour is gonna push people away

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: And you might be alone forever.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I’ve missed you, you know?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: We used to hang out all the time as kids.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Before everything happened...

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Don’t you want to go back to those days?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I just feel like I’m supposed to look out for you, set you on the right path in life.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: And that I screwed up by not being around.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: ...Your dad told me to take care of you...

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: But you’re not making it easy.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Your bone structure’s bad, your hair is terrible and you look like shit in general.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re a little bitch of a man, Louis!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I’ve always thought you were the most pathetic of us cousins.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I mean, look at yourself, Louis!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Even I’ve got my shit together more than you!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re just causing all your own problems!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I don’t know why I ever tried with you, Louis.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You are as sad, pathetic and nauseating to be around as always.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: How you turned out like this with parents like yours is a mystery I’ll never understand.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: ... who knows, maybe I’m not the only one who’s father isn’t why we thought.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LOUIS used 4CHAN INSULT

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's embarassingly effective!

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: You’re just upset because you’ve got beta, true-cel, prey eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: Negative cantal-tilt, lesser-Melvin ass, bitch.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: What does that even mean?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LOUIS used REDDIT CONSPIRACY

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: Outside? That’s where the satellites can reach you.

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: Look, just...

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: pulls out phone*

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: The government uses satellites to watch you.

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: We’re in a surveillance state! Who knows what kinds of brainwashing those lizards can do.

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: Look at this stuff!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Louis, this is all American stuff.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: We are in Australia.

dialogue.speaker: Louis
dialogue.text: Whatever, same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LOUIS used HYPER-SPECIFIC SLURS

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Jesus, where did you learn all that?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re literally Vietnamese too!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I don’t even know what some of these mean!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LOUIS used JORDAN PETERSON’S SELF-HELP BOOK

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LOUIS healed (he did not)

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Louis, man, I’m gonna be real. This is kind of sad.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I mean, what happened? Last I saw you, you were a real cheerful kid...

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: I don’t know. I grew up? The world kind of sucks, if you haven’t noticed.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: And people are the worst.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: They’re shallow and judgemental and real fucking open about how I don’t meet their standards.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: ... like you.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I’m not trying to be judgemental here, but look at yourself.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You look like you haven’t slept or seen the sun in years, and instead of doing anything useful you’re bashing your own face in and talking about ‘prey eyes’ or whatever.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I don’t mean to be rude, but you kind of... seem like you could use some advice.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I don’t know, man... I’m kind of worried about you...

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Oh, you’re so nice, aren’t you? What a real turn around from the shit you were saying before.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Do you feel better about yourself?

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Have you deemed me enough of a disappointment that you don’t feel threatened anymore?

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: You can turn around and pat yourself on the back for being such a good person and helping out poor, pathetic Louis.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Why? Feeling guilty for never fucking being around?

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I don’t know what you’re talking about.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: You don’t know? Could have used your ‘advice’ a couple years back.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Would’ve been helpful after dad died.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ... I’m sorry. I just... didn’t really know what to say.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I was going through some shit myself... didn’t really... think about you.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ... kind of thought Thi Há would have it covered.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Or Caspian.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Mum wasn’t exactly in the best place herself.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: And Caspian was fine, but...

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: You’ve always had this weird idea that Caspian is some super-human, perfect person...

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: ... he’s just a guy. A really awkward, kind of condescending and not particularly fun to talk to guy.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Plus he’s older... it’s not like we ever talked much before.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: It would’ve been nice to talk to you.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Without you around, there weren’t really any other guys I could talk to.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I’m sorry... I didn’t...

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ... I guess uncle Quân is a bit too intimidating to talk about most life problems with.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: And twelve is not a good age to be set adrift in...

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Fifteen is hardly a better age, but my dad didn’t actually die...

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You had to ask about a lot of... teenage insecurity stuff online, I guess.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: You’re saying that like it’s some terrible tragedy.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: I’m fine now, no thanks to you.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I’m not saying you’re not fine.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I’m just saying we can talk about stuff now, if you want.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: I don’t need to talk to you about anything anymore!

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: I have people to talk to now.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I meant if you wanted to talk to real people.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ... who aren’t on 4chan.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: You know what, fuck you!

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: You think you can just waltz back into my life five years after you left me in a fucking ditch and get all high and mighty about how I got myself out?

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I’m not trying to fight right now, Louis!

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Oh wait, I guess you do think that!

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Because you’re still just waiting around, open armed, for YOUR dad to walk back into your life like he didn’t ditch you without a second thought.

intermission-50%.speaker: Louis
intermission-50%.text: Newsflash, moron, you’re the only one waiting around for some loser that’s NEVER GONNA FUCKING SHOW!

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ... maybe I do remember why I never talk to you anymore.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: I thought you were the coolest, back then, y’know.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: The others were all so much older that I barely interacted with them, Caspian always seemed like he was too busy to indulge me... but you...

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: You’re such an asshole.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: How could you just say nothing?

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: My dad just died and you avoided me like the plague.

win.speaker: You
win.text: ... it wasn't about you.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Which is a shit thing to say, but it’s the truth. I was avoiding everyone.

win.speaker: You
win.text: The divorce... how it all went down... got to me.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Way more than it should have, I guess, since Caspian is fine...

win.speaker: You
win.text: You’re not wrong, when you said it’s like I’m still waiting for him to come back.

win.speaker: You
win.text: And I was worse, back then.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Way worse.

win.speaker: You
win.text: But I am sorry that me... being like that... happened at such a bad time for you.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: ... You’re such a coward.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: You’re sorry you ‘being like that happened at a bad time’?

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Just say you’re sorry for being an asshole.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Say you’re sorry for being an absolute dick and acting like you’re the centre of the universe and all your problems outweigh anyone else.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: ... admit that you just didn’t care.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Admit that you didn’t care about me, or my dad, or anyone but yourself.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Take some fucking responsibility for it.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I cared but... not enough, I guess.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I still think you’re a loser now, and seriously need to touch grass, but...

win.speaker: You
win.text: You didn’t deserve to be ignored, back then.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: You’re still an asshole, but... not as much of one I guess.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: And everyone’s a bit of a dick. Humanity is terrible, people are the worst.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: My bad for giving you shit about your dad too. I know the situation with you and him was... rough.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: ... I don’t really remember your dad, to be honest.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Didn’t have much interaction with him as a kid.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Most of what I know is just paraphrased from the rest of the family, and they’re... unreliable narrators at times...

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: But If mine was capable of it, I’d be hoping he’d come back too...

win.speaker: You
win.text: I’m sorry I wasn’t around when he died.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I was caught up in my own shit, and it was bad shit, but somehow you ended up getting the brunt of my bad handling of it.

win.speaker: You
win.text: And you fell into some... interesting ideas in the meantime.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: It’s not like I don’t know what people think about all this looks-maxxing, conspiracy stuff, I just...

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: I don’t know.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: I just feel... bad. Like, all the time.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: And misery loves company, right?

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: It’s nice to be part of something.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: It’s a whole lot of people trying to get better, at the heart of it.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: I don’t want to always be like this.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Louis, did you ever, like, see someone? After your dad dies?

win.speaker: You
win.text: A psychologist or something.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: The school gave me a counselling session, once.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: But you know mum believes more in, like, spiritual crystal healing and meditation than therapy.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Seems to have worked wonders for her.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Besides... it’s not like I don’t know what’s wrong with me already.

win.speaker: You
win.text: ... I didn’t see anyone about the whole... thing... with my parents either.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Looking back, maybe I really should have.

win.speaker: You
win.text: Caspian’s been trying to convince me to go, recently...

win.speaker: You
win.text: Maybe you should consider it too.

win.speaker: Louis
win.text: Maybe.

lose.speaker: Louis
lose.text: I used to think you were so cool back then, Y’know?

lose.speaker: Louis
lose.text: But now I realise you’re just some sad, beta male, cuck.

lose.speaker: Louis
lose.text: Oh, sorry, you’re not-real dad, who got actually cucked so bad that you exist.

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: You’re such a fucking bastard now Louis.

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: You don’t know anything about me, or my dad or that entire situation.

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: You were fucking twelve when it all went down, you don’t remember shit!

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: Nicest, most kind and understanding man in the world.

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: And then you turn out like this.

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: If he could only see you now,

lose.speaker: Louis
lose.text: Ironic of you to call me a bastard.

lose.speaker: Louis
lose.text: At least I know my dad loved me to his dying breath.

lose.speaker: Louis
lose.text: Everybody in this family knows yours doesn’t give a shit about you.

lose.speaker: Louis
lose.text: May as well go kill yourself.
